text.title: go-error-handling Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-error-handling as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Returning package-level mutable *AppError vars directly (e.g., return apperror.ErrNotFound).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Any caller can mutate the shared var: ErrNotFound.Message = "X" corrupts the global for all subsequent requests. Always use constructors.

failure-mode.description: Using %v instead of %w in fmt.Errorf: fmt.Errorf("op failed: %v", err).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: %v formats the error string but does not chain the error. errors.Is and errors.As return false through %v-wrapped errors. The errorlint linter catches this.

failure-mode.description: Type asserting instead of errors.As: appErr := err.(*apperror.AppError).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Type assertion fails for wrapped errors — it only checks the outermost type. errors.As traverses the Unwrap chain and handles wrapped *AppError correctly.

failure-mode.description: Wrap always sets HTTPStatus 500, silently promoting 4xx errors to 5xx.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Wrapping a NewNotFound (404) with a naive Wrap function produces an AppError with HTTPStatus 500. Wrap must preserve the inner AppError's status when present.

failure-mode.description: Leaking driver errors (pgx.ErrNoRows, sql.ErrNoRows) to HTTP handlers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Driver errors couple response shapes to the persistence layer. Map at the repository boundary: pgx.ErrNoRows → apperror.NewNotFound(...).

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A write failure leaves the client without a response body. Log the write error even if you cannot re-send the response. errcheck (golangci-lint) flags this.
